title: ranunculus peltatus subsp. sphaerospermus
intro: Ranunculus peltatus subsp. sphaerospermus, also known as the round-fruited water-crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, with its roots and rhizomes containing ranunculin, a compound with potential medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and fever, and its health benefits may include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The herbal preparations of Ranunculus peltatus subsp. sphaerospermus typically involve drying and processing the roots and rhizomes, which can be used as a tincture, tea, or infusement.
